business.headline: Kotak Mahindra to buy Deutsche Bank's retail business in India for $30 millionThe acquisition will help Kotak expand its presence among retail and affluent customers, while Deutsche Bank will focus on its core business areas.

UK says it's minded to intervene in $110-billion Paramount-Warner Bros mergerUK Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy said the proposed merger could have implications for Britain's media landscape and that she was considering intervention on public interest grounds.

Govt extends customs duty relief on key petrochemical imports until July 15Although the situation in West Asia has started to improve, the government has chosen to continue the customs duty exemption for a short period to help industries adjust without facing sudden cost pressures.

Noel Tata to step down as Voltas Chairman, announces decision at AGMDuring the AGM, Noel Tata said the company has built a strong leadership team by promoting talent from within and bringing in experienced professionals from outside.
Govt extends CBDT chairman Ravi Agrawal's term by six monthsRavi Agrawal's new term will begin on July 1, 2026, and continue till December 31, 2026, or until further orders, whichever comes earlier.

Sensex ends 250 points lower, Nifty below 23,900; Maruti Suzuki gains 5%The BSE Sensex fell 249.70 points, or 0.33%, to close at 76,478.67, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 80.50 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 23,865.75.

Centre pushes GST Appellate Tribunal appeal filing deadline by 1 monthThe new deadline is July 31, 2026, replacing the earlier deadline of June 30, 2026, which had been notified in September last year.

Indians love gold. So why is there a rush to sell it?Gold still occupies a special place in Indian households, but rising prices and changing investor behaviour are transforming it from a family heirloom into a modern financial asset.

8th Pay Commission: Salary and allowance data to be submitted ahead of pay revisionThe information is expected to help the Commission prepare recommendations on revised pay scales, allowances, overall government expenditure and long-term financial sustainability.

Filed your ITR? Complete this step within 30 days of filing or it may be rejectedClicked 'Submit' on your ITR? Don't relax just yet. You still need to complete one crucial step to make your tax return valid. Have a look.

Guaranteed returns, zero risk: How $60 million investment scam fooled 300 peoplePaul Regan pleaded guilty after prosecutors accused him of running a $60 million Ponzi scheme that drew more than 300 US investors with promises of guaranteed returns and zero risk. The case shows how emotional reassurance, false protection claims and trust can override basic questions about investment risk.

Sensex, Nifty erase early gains as IT stocks drag; Q1 earnings in focusBenchmark indices erased opening gains on Tuesday as IT stocks pulled the market lower. Attention is now shifting to June-quarter earnings, with analysts watching financials, healthcare and power.

Gold prices see biggest monthly drop in 18 years. Good time to buy?Gold prices have slid sharply from record highs as investors weigh higher US rates and a stronger dollar. The correction has reopened the buying debate, with upcoming US data likely to shape the next move.

Microsoft loses $570 billion in a month. Is Wall Street losing faith in AI boom?Microsoft has lost more than $570 billion in market value this month, marking its worst monthly performance since the dot-com era. Investors are increasingly questioning whether the software giant's massive AI investments will generate enough profits to justify the spending.

Japan's yen falls to 40-year low against US dollarThe yen fell to its weakest level against the US dollar since 1986, reviving expectations of fresh market intervention by Tokyo. The slide reflects the widening US-Japan rate gap and keeps pressure on Japan's economy and global investors.

Old vs new tax regime: How standard deduction works and who benefitsAs the ITR filing season gathers pace, our 'KNOW YOUR TAX' series breaks down key tax benefits. In the first part, we explain the standard deduction and how it works under the old and new tax regimes.

HDFC Bank appoints ex-poll body chief Rajiv Kumar as chairman; RBI nod awaitedHDFC Bank has appointed former Finance Secretary and ex-Chief Election Commissioner Rajiv Kumar as its new chairman, subject to RBI approval, replacing Atanu Chakraborty.
Yen sinks to 40-year low, dollar slips but stays near 13-month highThe Japanese yen weakened to its lowest levels against the US currency since 1986.

Govt to lift petrol and diesel sales curbs from July 1 as supply concerns easeThe precautionary measures were introduced to protect domestic fuel supplies at a time when uncertainty in the global energy market was increasing.

From ITR deadlines to passport fees: 5 financial changes from July 1 you must knowWhether you're planning to file your ITR, apply for a passport or simply keep track of your finances, July 1 brings a few important changes you shouldn't ignore. Here are five key financial updates that could matter to you. Which ones will affect you the most?

entertainment.headline: Timothee Chalamet and Selena Gomez voice rocket scientists in Not Alone trailer Illumination has released the first trailer for Not Alone, starring Timothee Chalamet and Selena Gomez. The preview sets up Chalamet's first animated voice role alongside a sci-fi chase involving three runaway aliens.

Actor Ansiba Hassan defamation case: FIR against Tini Tom over communal remarks An Ernakulam magistrate court has directed police to register a case against Tini Tom on actor Ansiba Hassan's complaint. The order follows her allegations of defamation and communal slurs.

Love is still the same: Gaurav Khanna breaks silence as Akanksha announces divorce Actor Gaurav Khanna has broken his silence after Akanksha Chamola confirmed their divorce on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa. Here's what the actor said and why the couple decided to part ways.

Spider-Man Tom Holland takes Lionel Messi web-swinging in new video. Watch Sony Pictures has unveiled a surprise Spider-Man: Brand New Day video featuring Tom Holland's Peter Parker and Lionel Messi. Here's why the crossover has everyone talking.

Paer pe girke: Ssunita Ahuja reveals how Kashmera Shah mended family feud After years of estrangement, actor Govinda's wife Ssunita Ahuja revealed that Kashmera Shah apologised with tears during their emotional reunion, bringing the family's 14-year feud to an end.

Gaurav Khanna makes first post after wife Akanksha Chamola announces divorce Actor Gaurav Khanna shared a social media post after Akanksha Chamola announced their divorce on Lock Upp Season 2. Her remarks indicated they remain on speaking terms but differ on their future, including having children.

Half-yearly box office report: Dhurandhar leads a mixed report card at the movies Half-yearly box office report 2026: Dhurandhar dominates as trade experts analyse winners, flops, Hollywood's impact and what lies ahead.

Exclusive | Kunal Kemmu shares update on Golmaal 5, confirms Madgaon Express sequel In an exclusive interview with India Today, Kunal Kemmu shared updates on Golmaal 5 and Madgaon Express 2. He said the Golmaal film is being shot, while the sequel to his directorial debut is in the works.

Zeenat Aman says Bollywood reduced her to 'gayegi, nachegi, bheegegi' Actor Zeenat Aman said in an interview that her on-screen sex-symbol image never matched her real personality. She also recalled that filmmakers in the 1970s prioritised her appearance over her creative input.

Alia Bhatt's Alpha gets U/A 16+ certificate after CBFC modifies violent scenes Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Alpha has received its CBFC clearance ahead of release, but not before several changes were made. Here's what was modified and why.

Don't like India's Got Latent: CJP's Abhijeet Dipke calls Samay Raina privileged Cockroach Janata Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has said he doesn't like India's Got Latent and would never appear on Samay Raina's show. Calling the comedian "privileged," his remarks have triggered reactions online, with many pointing to his own educational background.

Spider-Man 5, Black Panther 3 and Shang-Chi 2 lead Marvel's future plans: Report Spider-Man 5, Black Panther 3 and Shang-Chi 2 are reportedly among Marvel Studios' biggest priorities after Avengers: Secret Wars. Here's what the latest report reveals about the MCU's future.

Akanksha Chamola's 'red forest' remark about Gaurav Khanna resurfaces after split An old interview in which Akanksha Chamola jokingly called Gaurav Khanna "a red forest" has resurfaced after she confirmed their separation. The clip has drawn fresh attention as fans revisit their past exchanges after news of the divorce.

Did Sunny Deol wear worn-out shoes at Ikka trailer launch? Viral video sparks buzz Sunny Deol's appearance at the Ikka trailer launch went viral after eagle-eyed paparazzi spotted his worn-out shoes. The clip sparked reactions online, with some trolling the actor while others praised his simplicity.

Actor Khushbu tears up as daughter gets married, holds husband Sundar C in joy Actor-politician Khushbu Sundar turned emotional as daughter Avantika married Shravan Sreenivasan in an intimate Goa ceremony last week. A video from the event with Khushbu wiping her tears in joy surfaced online.

Ramayana tops IMDb's list of most anticipated films of 2026; King, Alpha follow IMDb has revealed its list of the most anticipated Indian films for the second half of 2026, featuring major releases across Bollywood and regional cinema. From Ramayana Part 1 and Alpha to King and Mirzapur: The Movie, here's a look at the titles generating the biggest buzz.

Inside Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding with NDAs, guest list and more Singer-songwriter Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce's rumoured wedding has sparked fresh speculation after reports revealed unusual privacy measures and elaborate planning. Here's what is being claimed.

You humbly knelt by my mother: Shanthnu Bhagyaraj thanks CM Vijay in emotional note Actor Shanthnu Bhagyaraj thanked Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay for standing by his family after K Bhagyaraj's death and ensuring state honours at the funeral. His note also linked a personal memory from his wedding.

Triptii Dimri was my biggest source of support on Maa Behen: Actor Dharna Durga Dharna Durga said Triptii Dimri became a key support during the shoot of Maa Behen. She also said the Netflix film's response and audience connection have surpassed her expectations.

general news.headline: Pak warns world over Indus Waters Treaty at global conferencePakistan organised an international conference on Indus Waters and warned that if the treaty failed no world order that was on paper would remain secure. India put the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance after the terror attack in Pahalgam. Pakistan has since been desperate.

PM speaks to Iran President, stresses peace, freedom of navigation in West AsiaDuring the conversation, PM Modi welcomed the understanding reached and reiterated India's longstanding position that all disputes should be resolved through dialogue and diplomacy.

Did you check Siya's background? Ketan's question to family days before his murderKetan also complained that Siya's phone was often engaged whenever he called her and that she frequently mentioned Chetan during their conversations.

Jai Hind from an ethanol guinea pigIndia's biggest experiment isn't happening in a laboratory. It's happening at fuel pumps, with 1.4 billion people as the sample. Welcome to the world's biggest fuel trial, where every Indian motorist is unwillingly, a participant.

US Supreme Court strikes down Trump order restricting birthright citizenshipIn a major blow to President Donald Trump's immigration agenda, the US Supreme Court struck down his order limiting birthright citizenship and reaffirmed law granting citizenship to nearly all children born in America.

Does India's delegation for Khamenei funeral reveal a shift?A junior Union minister and a governor will be travelling to Iran to represent India at the funeral of former Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iran had invited PM Modi. Does the selection of the two representatives hint at a shift in New Delhi's Middle East diplomacy?

Hardik Pandya shifts to Bengaluru, makes BCCI facility permanent training baseHardik Pandya has relocated to Bengaluru to train regularly at the BCCI's Centre of Excellence. The move marks an unusual long-term shift as he continues rehabilitation from injury and plans his white-ball future.

Siya Goyal's 'lawyer' sues her brother for Rs 10 crore over defamationAdvocate Aashutosh Srivastava has sent a Rs 10 crore defamation notice to Sahil Goyal, accusing the brother of murder accused Siya Goyal of making false statements. Srivastava's notice was in response to Sahil Goyal saying that the lawyer had used deception to get Siya to sign legal documents.

Only AC government buses in Tamil Nadu from now, CM Vijay says: Transport MinisterTransport Minister Vijay Tamilan Parthiban said Tamil Nadu will henceforth procure only AC buses since Chief Minister Vijay wants people to travel more widely and comfortably.

Watch LIVE: Two astronauts repairing Space Station flying at 28,000 kmphTwo Nasa astronauts stepped outside the International Space Station to replace a faulty Canadarm2 wrist joint. The repair is aimed at restoring a vital robotic arm used for cargo operations, maintenance and astronaut support.

Fed up with abuse, woman kills drunk husband, stages heart attack to hide murderAfter the incident, the accused reportedly panicked and called relatives, claiming her husband had suffered a heart attack and was not responding. With their help, she rushed him to a government hospital in Beed, where doctors declared him dead.

PM chairs 4-hour meeting with top bureaucrats; pushes deregulation, self-relianceThe meeting, one of the first full-secretary-level interactions chaired by the PM in the current financial year, was aimed to align the bureaucracy with government's policy priorities for the second half of 2026.

India to get below-normal rain in July, El Nino to grip monsoonThe IMD has forecast below-normal rainfall across India in July as El Niño strengthens over the equatorial Pacific. The outlook follows a 39% June rainfall deficit, making it the country's fifth-driest June since 1901.

US Supreme Court upholds bans on trans women in female school, college sportsIn a significant 6-3 majority, the US Supreme Court upheld laws in Idaho and West Virginia that bar trans women and girls from competing on female sports teams in schools and colleges, clearing the way for states to enforce similar restrictions on transgender student athletes.

Lahore tuition centre roof collapse kills 14 children, contractor heldA roof collapse at a private tuition centre in Lahore killed 14 children and injured 20 others. The tragedy has triggered a criminal probe into unsafe construction and school safety oversight.

Man accused of drugging, raping woman with AIDS acquitted after she disappearsThe court held that police failed to establish the allegations beyond doubt, citing the absence of the complainant's testimony, lack of identification evidence and insufficient material to prove rape.

A 64-year-old intern went viral. Is India retiring its smartest talent too soon?A viral video of a 64-year-old intern has reignited debate over retirement in India. As people live longer and healthier lives, should retirement become a choice instead of a fixed age? Experts say the country's untapped silver workforce could become one of its biggest economic and social strengths.

Satellite captures 1,500-km-long monsoon rain band from Bengal to KashmirSatellite imagery has captured a monsoon trough stretching from the northern Bay of Bengal to Jammu and Kashmir. Its expected southward shift could boost thunderstorms and help the monsoon advance across north India.

Shashank Singh denies assault allegations: Cook was taking private videos Punjab Kings cricketer Shashank Singh has denied allegations of assault and wrongful confinement made by his former cook. Noisy Mexico fans ruin Ecuador's sleep ahead of knockout clashHundreds of Mexico supporters gathered outside Ecuador's team hotel in Mexico City ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 32 clash against the co-hosts. The rowdy fans created noise with chants, horns and speakers in an apparent attempt to disrupt the visitors' sleep.

Defiant Koeman blasts reporters after Netherlands crash out in Round of 32Netherlands coach Ronald Koeman launched a strong defence of his tactical approach despite his side's FIFA World Cup Round of 32 exit against Morocco. Netherlands crashed out of the tournament after failing to convert in the penalty shootouts.

The light-hearted moment followed a testing four-set victory over Wu Yibing in the opening round.

Penalties haunt Netherlands once again as Dutch crash out in R32Penalty shootouts haunted Netherlands once again as Ronald Koeman's team were knocked out of the FIFA World Cup 2026. A disastrous spell of spot kicks saw them miss 3 chances, sealing their fate in the tournament.

Explained: Did VAR call send Germany crashing out of FIFA World Cup?FIFA World Cup 2026: Germany believed Jonathan Tah had sent them through to the Round of 16 before VAR intervened dramatically in extra time. The overturned goal sparked fury, scrutiny of goalkeeper protection, and another painful early tournament exit for the four-time champions.

Harmanpreet Kaur to captain India as BCCI announces Asian Games squadHarmanpreet Kaur will continue to lead India in T20Is. The BCCI on Tuesday announced a 15-member squad for women's cricket at Asian Games 2026, scheduled to be held in Aichi-Nagoya, Japan, in September 2026.

FIR against cricketer Shashank Singh and retired IPS father for assaulting cookPunjab Kings player Shashank Singh, his retired IPS father and their driver have been booked after their cook alleged assault and abuse in Bhopal. The complaint includes claims of phone seizure and visible injuries, with police now investigating the case.

Australia eye another final as West Indies plot ambushAustralia enter the Women's T20 World Cup semi-final as overwhelming favourites after an unbeaten campaign, but a resilient West Indies side will back its experienced match-winners to spring a knockout upset.

Paraguay celebrates historic World Cup win vs Germany with public holidayParaguay's stunning World Cup win over Germany has sparked nationwide celebrations, with President Santiago Pena declaring a public holiday to mark La Albirroja's historic Round of 16 qualification.

Gakpo in tears, scoring days after unborn son's death, teammates rally around himFIFA World Cup 2026: Cody Gakpo scored for the Netherlands in their soul-crushing defeat to Morocco in the Round of 32 game, days after his partner announced the loss of their unborn son. The goal sparked an emotional response and drew support from teammates and fans.

Morocco 3-2 Netherlands in penalties: Disastrous Dutch crash out of World CupNetherlands (NED) vs Morocco (MAR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: A disastrous penalty shootout sees Netherlands crash out of the FIFA World Cup. 3 misses from the Dutch saw Morocco reach the Round of 16, where they will face co-hosts Canada. Paraguay's finest hour: Giants Germany sent packing in Round of 32Paraguay held Germany for 120 minutes before winning 4-3 in the shootout after a 1-1 draw. The result handed Germany their first World Cup shootout defeat and gave Paraguay a landmark victory.

Neymar hits back at German economist who predicted Japan would beat BrazilNeymar took a playful swipe at German economist Joachim Klement after Brazil's 2-1 win over Japan proved his World Cup prediction wrong, with the Brazil star's post quickly going viral.

Djokovic sees off Wu Yibing in Round 1 as proposal adds to Wimbledon spectacleNovak Djokovic overcame a spirited Wu Yibing in four sets to reach the Wimbledon second round, with a surprise marriage proposal in the stands and his witty request for a wedding invitation stealing attention.

Why did Neymar not play in the Round of 32 clash?Brazil vs Japan: Carlo Ancelotti revealed Neymar was kept on the bench against Japan as a contingency for extra time, with Brazil's tactical halftime switch inspiring a dramatic 2-1 comeback to reach the Round of 16.

Japan coach, players bow down to fans in apology as Brazil end World Cup dreamFIFA World Cup 2026: Japan coach Hajime Moriyasu and his players bowed before their fans after Brazil's dramatic 2-1 win ended the Samurai Blue's FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign in an emotional Round of 32 farewell.

West Indies have nothing to lose, will be fearless vs Australia: Hayley MatthewsWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, AUS vs WI: West Indies skipper Hayley Matthews said that her team will be fearless when they face Sophie Molineux's Australia on Tuesday in the first semifinal at the Oval.

Don Carlo's big-game magic sends Brazil past brave Japan into Round of 16FIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil came from behind to beat a brave and well-organised Japan side 2-1 in Houston, sealing their place in the World Cup Round of 16. Australia wary of Hayley Matthews, Deandra Dottin threats in semis vs West IndiesWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, AUS vs WI: Ash Gardner said that Australia need to be careful with West Indies having match-winners like Deandra Dottin and Hayley Matthews going into the semis on Tuesday at the Oval.

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
